title: The Case For US Bank Stocks
text:
The Case For US Bank Stocks

Tan Kai Xian

Gavekal Research | 6 Jul 2026

While the S&P 500 index has moved sideways since June 1, the S&P 500 Banks index has gained a robust 13%. This marks a sharp reversal from the first five months of the year, when a string of negative headlines caused US bank stocks to underperform the broader equity index by 15pp. The recent outperformance suggests investors’ concerns are easing. More importantly, the fundamental backdrop for US banks has strengthened considerably.Three concerns weighed on US bank stocks during the first five months of the year despite the country’s robust economic growth. First, President Donald Trump’s proposal on January 10 to cap credit card interest rates at 10% for a year unsettled investors, who feared it could mark the start of a broader push for policies detrimental to the banking sector. Although the proposal ultimately stalled after opposition from Congressional Republicans and the banking industry (see The Odd One Out), the uncertainty lingered. Second, mounting stress in the US private credit market raised fears of contagion spreading to commercial banks. Third, the spike in oil prices following the outbreak of the Iran war increased the risk of US rate hikes, which would have squeezed bank profitability by pushing deposit costs higher. With all three concerns now largely behind investors, US bank stocks have rebounded.
